title: Aegis Statusline Preset Descriptor
description: One statusline.json per preset folder under statuslines/<name>/. Lean 5-field surface frontmatter expressed as top-level JSON keys, plus statusline-specific config (theme, segments, mergeGroups, refreshIntervalSeconds).

name.description: Preset folder name (kebab-case). Must match the containing folder.

platforms.description: Hosts that render this preset. At v0.0.4 only `claude` has a real projection; other hosts are honest gaps. Preset content stays host-agnostic so a future hook can render it.

source.description: Provenance string for migrated/derived presets, e.g. 'claude-hud:Essential'.

theme.description: Name of a palette under statuslines/_shared/themes/<theme>.json (e.g. mono, default, tokyo-night, gruvbox-dark).

segments.description: Ordered status lines. Each element is one rendered line: an ordered array of segment IDs joined left-to-right. Multiple elements render as multiple lines (top to bottom).

separator.description: OPTIONAL. The inter-segment separator GLYPH (e.g. '·' or '|'), NOT including surrounding spaces — the runtime wraps it in a single space on each side and colorizes it with the theme 'separator' key. Default '·' when absent, matching pre-existing behavior.

mergeGroups.description: OPTIONAL (honored since v0.0.14). Each inner array lists segment IDs that render with NO separator between them (a tight visual group, joined by `mergeSeparator` — default empty string). A segment may appear in at most one group. Segments not in any group keep the normal separator. Ordering of the group within a line follows the first member's position in that line's `segments` array.

mergeSeparator.description: OPTIONAL (v0.0.14). String inserted BETWEEN members of a mergeGroup (default "" — no separator). E.g. "/" to render git+pr as `main/#247`.

order.description: OPTIONAL (v0.0.14). Explicit segment-priority list. When present, every line's segments are STABLE-SORTED by their index in this list (segments absent from `order` keep their relative position, sorted after listed ones). Additive: omit to keep the authored `segments` order. Does not add or remove segments — only reorders within each line.

thresholds.description: OPTIONAL (v0.0.14). Threshold escalation: per-metric ordered breakpoints that map a numeric value to a theme color key, so a segment can escalate (e.g. context usage green → warning → critical). The runtime resolves the matching color key and exposes it to segments via `ctx.threshold(metric, value)`; segments opt in to honoring it. Existing presets omit this and render exactly as before.

additionalProperties.description: Ordered breakpoints for one metric, evaluated top-to-bottom; the FIRST whose `at` is <= value wins. List from highest `at` to lowest.

color.description: Theme color KEY (resolved against the active palette's colors.*, e.g. 'warning', 'critical').

label.description: OPTIONAL severity label a segment may surface (e.g. 'HIGH').

i18n.description: OPTIONAL (v0.0.14). Localizable labels. The runtime exposes `ctx.t(key)` returning `labels[key]` (falling back to the key itself). Additive: presets without i18n render English/literal labels exactly as before.

locale.description: BCP-47-ish locale tag (e.g. 'en', 'pt-BR'). Advisory metadata; the runtime keys off `labels` directly.

labels.description: Label-key → localized string. Keys are segment-defined label slugs (e.g. 'ctx', 'cost').

refreshIntervalSeconds.description: Set ONLY for presets containing time-based segments (usage countdown, session-time, prompt-cache TTL, pr review-state drift). Event-driven presets omit this. 0 or absent = event-only.
